roadmap.goal: Implement the flight-booking-system feature end-to-end across 8 elephant-carpaccio slices per DISTILL artifacts.
roadmap.crafter: nw-software-crafter (substituted with alf-atdd-developer for this orchestration)
roadmap.methodology: Red-Green-Refactor (5-phase TDD) with walking skeleton first

phases.name: Slice 01 — Walking Skeleton (one end-to-end booking via real HTTP)

steps.name: Money value object and domain-model constructors with Decimal arithmetic
criteria: Money.of('299') returns Decimal('299.00') quantized to 2dp with ROUND_HALF_EVEN.
Money equality is value-based; Money + Money and Money * Decimal return new Money.
Flight, Cabin, Seat, Booking, Quote, PriceBreakdown, PassengerDetails dataclass constructors accept the values used by the walking-skeleton @given background step without raising.
__SCAFFOLD__ marker removed from money.py.
steps.scenario_name: Search, book, retrieve — end-to-end through HTTP

steps.name: In-memory adapters, mock payment/email/audit, composition root for tests
criteria: InMemoryFlightRepository.add and .get are functional; .search returns matches for origin/destination/departure-date.
InMemoryBookingRepository.save and .get round-trip a Booking.
MockPaymentGateway.charge(token='mock-ok', amount) returns succeeded=True; token='fail' returns succeeded=False.
MockEmailSender.queue_confirmation appends to .queued list.
InMemoryAuditLog.write appends to .events list.
build_test_container(now, audit_path=None, deterministic_ids=True) returns a wired Container with all services; no __SCAFFOLD__ remaining in those adapter files or wire.py.
steps.scenario_name: Search, book, retrieve — end-to-end through HTTP

steps.name: FastAPI search + book + get-booking routes turning the WS scenario GREEN
criteria: GET /flights/search returns 200 with matching flights; walking-skeleton scenario step assert on flight id passes.
POST /bookings with valid body returns 201 with a bookingReference and status='CONFIRMED'.
GET /bookings/{reference} returns 200 with flightId and seats list.
Confirmation email is queued; audit log receives a BookingCommitted event.
Running `pytest -m walking_skeleton` is GREEN.
JsonlAuditLog adapter-integration scenario also passes (writes 3 events to tmp_path, reads back 3 events).
steps.scenario_name: Search, book, retrieve — end-to-end through HTTP

phases.name: Slice 02 — Flight catalog search with pagination and validation

steps.name: Seed a realistic catalog (200+ flights, 20 routes, 5 airlines) and filter by origin/destination/date
criteria: A seeded catalog provides ≥200 flights across ≥20 routes, ≥5 airlines, ≥30 dates, 3 classes.
GET /flights/search?origin=LAX&destination=NYC&departureDate=2026-06-01 returns only matching flights.
An empty match returns 200 with an empty results array and pagination metadata total=0.
steps.scenario_name: Filter by origin, destination, and departure date

steps.name: Pagination defaults + max + input validation (per-field 400 errors)
criteria: Page size defaults to 20; size>20 is clamped to 20.
Invalid IATA, invalid date, passengers outside 1..9, unknown class enum each produce a 400 with a per-field error list.
p95 of 100 sequential searches on the seeded catalog is under 500ms.
steps.scenario_name: Pagination defaults and maximum

phases.name: Slice 03 — Seat map and seat-specific commit

steps.name: Default 30x6 cabin fixture with class/kind per seat (ADR-004)
criteria: Default cabin factory builds 180 seats (30 rows × 6 columns A-F).
Rows 1-2 are FIRST, rows 3-6 are BUSINESS, rows 7-30 are ECONOMY.
Row 14 is marked EXIT_ROW; B/E seats are MIDDLE across Economy; row 3 A/F are WINDOW_SUITE.
Seat map assertion in milestone-03 scenario 'Seat map returns 180 seats with correct class assignment' passes.
steps.scenario_name: Seat map returns 180 seats with correct class assignment

steps.name: GET /flights/{id}/seats endpoint + SeatMapService composing flight, bookings, locks
criteria: GET /flights/{id}/seats returns 180 entries with seatId, class, kind, status.
After a successful booking of '12C', a subsequent seat-map GET shows '12C' as OCCUPIED.
Flight-not-found → 404.
steps.scenario_name: Seat map reflects a newly-committed booking

steps.name: Seat validation on commit (unknown, OCCUPIED, BLOCKED)
criteria: POST /bookings with a non-existent seatId → 400 with 'unknown seat'.
POST /bookings with OCCUPIED seat → 409 'seat already booked'.
POST /bookings with BLOCKED seat → 409 'seat not for sale'.
All three milestone-03 error scenarios pass.
steps.scenario_name: Booking a non-existent seat

steps.name: Pure pricing function with Appendix B tables (demand × time × day)
criteria: pricing.price(PricingInputs(base=299, occupancy=0, days=30, dow=TUE, surcharges=(), taxes=0, fees=0)) yields total=228.74 USD.
Appendix B example 2 yields 897.00 USD; example 3 yields 1944.00 USD (banker's rounding at the end).
hypothesis property test (≥1000 cases) confirms: positive totals, same-inputs→same-output, clock not referenced.
Boundary inputs (0/30/31%; 60/21-day cutoffs; each weekday) land in the correct bucket.
DEMAND_TABLE, TIME_TABLE, DOW_TABLE are module-level constants; changing a multiplier requires editing exactly one location.
steps.scenario_name: Appendix B example 1 — empty Tuesday flight, 30 days ahead

steps.name: Wire pricing into POST /quotes with demand/time/day context from flight state
criteria: POST /quotes returns baseFare, demandMultiplier, timeMultiplier, dayMultiplier, and total in the response body.
All three Appendix B example scenarios go GREEN with the exact documented totals.
Occupancy is computed from the flight's current (seats + bookings + active locks) state.
steps.scenario_name: Appendix B example 2 — 80% full Friday flight, 2 days ahead

phases.name: Slice 05 — Price breakdown with surcharges, taxes, and fees (Appendix A)

steps.name: Appendix A surcharge table — (class, kind) → Money lookup
criteria: SURCHARGES[(ECONOMY, EXIT_ROW)] == +35.00 USD; (ECONOMY, MIDDLE) == -5.00 USD; (BUSINESS, LIE_FLAT_SUITE) == +200.00 USD (three example rows cited — full table must be present).
Adding a seat in quote returns a seat_surcharges list entry for each seat.
milestone-05 scenarios for exit row, middle seat, lie-flat-suite all pass.
steps.scenario_name: Exit row economy seat surcharge (+$35)

steps.name: Domestic vs international taxes + baggage/meal fees
criteria: Flight carries a route_kind (domestic|international); taxes line equals configured rate × base.
Fees line includes baggage/meal/seat-selection fees per configurable table (this iteration: flat rates only).
Domestic and international scenarios in milestone-05 both pass.
steps.scenario_name: Domestic vs international tax rates

steps.name: Full price breakdown JSON (precision round-trips; total reproducible on paper)
criteria: Quote response body has baseFare, demandMultiplier, timeMultiplier, dayMultiplier, seatSurcharges[], taxes, fees, total, currency.
Every monetary value is a 2-decimal-place string; parsing back yields the same Decimal.
total = baseFare × demand × time × day + Σ seat_surcharges + taxes + fees, reproducible on paper from the response fields.
steps.scenario_name: Total equals the arithmetic of all components

phases.name: Slice 06 — Quote TTL and audit log

steps.name: QuoteStore with 30-min TTL; get_valid returns None for expired quotes
criteria: save(quote) stores it; get_valid(quote_id, now) returns the quote if now < expires_at, else None.
POST /quotes response contains quoteId and expiresAt exactly 30 minutes after created_at.
milestone-06 scenario 'Quote expires 30 minutes after creation' passes.
steps.scenario_name: Quote expires 30 minutes after creation

steps.name: Append-only audit log events (QuoteCreated, BookingCommitted, PaymentFailed)
criteria: QuoteCreated events include quote_id, session_id, full pricing inputs, computed total, created_at.
BookingCommitted events include booking_reference, quote_id, total_charged, committed_at.
PaymentFailed events include quote_id, reason, attempted_at.
InMemoryAuditLog and JsonlAuditLog both write events (the latter to tmp_path during adapter-integration test).
Replay check: for each BookingCommitted event, pricing.price replayed against the matching QuoteCreated inputs produces the same total (KPI-T3).
steps.scenario_name: Audit log replay reconstructs the committed total

steps.name: Commit honors quoted total; 410 on expired; 404 on unknown quote
criteria: BookingService.commit reads quote.total and charges that — never recomputes price.
Even if occupancy jumps between quote and commit, the committed total equals the quoted total (within TTL).
After TTL: POST /bookings returns 410 Gone with body citing 'quote expired'.
Unknown quoteId: POST /bookings returns 404.
KPI-T1 quote fidelity scenario passes; audit log contains matching QuoteCreated + BookingCommitted entries.
steps.scenario_name: Commit within 30 minutes honors the quoted total even if demand changed

steps.name: SeatLockStore.acquire atomic CAS primitive with 10-min TTL
criteria: SeatLockStore.acquire(flight_id, seat_ids, session_id, now) is atomic under the store's RLock.
If any target seat has a non-expired lock owned by a different session, acquire returns SeatLockConflict with the conflicting seats and installs nothing.
Otherwise it installs a LockRecord per seat with expires_at = now + 10 min.
Expired lock records are treated as free on next acquire.
POST /seat-locks with a free seat returns 201 with lockId and expiresAt.
steps.scenario_name: Single session acquires a lock on an available seat

steps.name: Commit transaction under lock + payment failure preserves lock
criteria: BookingService.commit requires a valid (unexpired, matching-session) seat lock; 410 on expired, 403 on session mismatch.
Commit runs under commit_lock (module-level RLock) and: reads quote → charges payment → installs OCCUPIED seat status → saves booking → releases lock → writes audit, all atomically.
Payment failure: audit PaymentFailed; no booking; lock remains valid until its own TTL.
Concurrent non-seat-overlapping bookings complete in parallel (KPI-P3: 10 concurrent successful bookings on different seats).
steps.scenario_name: Payment failure preserves the seat lock for retry

steps.name: Concurrency harness — 100 trials × 10 threads × 0 double-bookings (KPI-T2)
criteria: scripts/race_last_seat.py runs 100 trials; each trial has 10 threads attempting to lock the same last seat.
Every trial produces exactly 1 winner and 9 rejections; 0 double-bookings across all 100 trials.
The harness emits a JSON result summary that the CI gate asserts on.
milestone-07 scenario 'Race harness — 100 trials, zero double-bookings' passes when the harness is invoked from the test.
steps.scenario_name: Race harness — 100 trials, zero double-bookings

steps.name: Round-trip pairing — composed from two one-way searches with ≥2h buffer
criteria: GET /flights/search with returnDate returns paired outbound+return flights where return.origin == outbound.destination and return.departure ≥ outbound.arrival + 2h.
Pagination paginates pairs; metadata reports pairCount AND flightCount (= 2 × pairCount).
steps.scenario_name: Round-trip search returns compatible outbound+return pairs

steps.name: Filters — airline, minPrice/maxPrice, departureTimeFrom/To; AND-composed and commutative
criteria: airline filter restricts by IATA exact match.
minPrice/maxPrice filters on the indicative total (inclusive of surcharges).
departureTimeFrom/To filters on local-time window.
Filters compose (AND) and are commutative (swapping order yields identical result sets).
All 6 milestone-08 scenarios pass.
steps.scenario_name: Filters compose (AND) and are commutative

validation.notes: Roadmap populated by orchestrator (main Claude instance) because nw-solution-architect agent is not registered in the Task tool's subagent enum in this session. Content derived verbatim from DISCUSS + DESIGN + DISTILL artifacts already in the repo. Architecture decisions were NOT made here; they were made in DESIGN and this roadmap only translates them into TDD step boundaries.
